fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Ohio Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Ohio - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity ohio, how to transfer physical therapist license to ohio, physical therapist compact ohio, ohio physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Ohio OT, PT, and Athletic Trainers Board
board.mailing_address:
Ohio OTPTAT Board
77 S. High Street, 16th Floor
Columbus, OH 43215-6108

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education, including at least 2 hours of ethics, jurisprudence, or Ohio laws and rules, every 2 years

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Ohio.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Application
steps.description: Register for an account on the eLicense Ohio portal (elicense.ohio.gov) and complete the online application for 'Initial Physical Therapist Licensure' or 'Licensure by Endorsement', paying the required application fee.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your CAPTE-accredited physical therapy program to be sent directly to the Ohio OTPTAT Board. Other supporting documents may be uploaded directly to your eLicense Ohio application.

steps.title: Pass National & Jurisprudence Exams
steps.description: If applying by examination, register with the FSBPT and pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE). Additionally, complete and pass the Ohio Physical Therapy Jurisprudence Examination, which is typically administered online.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Records Background Check
steps.description: Submit to a criminal records background check through fingerprinting (BCI & FBI) as directed by the Board. Instructions for this process are provided within the eLicense Ohio application or on the Board's website.

steps.title: Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: Once all required documents, exam scores (NPTE and Jurisprudence), and background check results have been received and verified, the Ohio OTPTAT Board will review your complete application and issue your physical therapist license upon approval.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Ohio physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Ohio have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Ohio participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Ohio without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Ohio?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Ohio while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Ohio
hero.dek: Physical Therapist licensure in Ohio is managed by the Ohio Occupational Therapy, Physical Therapy, and Athletic Trainers Board, with all applications submitted through the eLicense Ohio portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To begin, navigate to the eLicense Ohio portal and click 'Register a New Account'. Follow the prompts to create your account by providing personal information, setting up security questions, and creating a username and password. Once registered, log in and select the 'Initial Physical Therapist Licensure' application type to start your application.
